response: Tymnet was an international data‑communications network, headquartered in San Jose, California, that used virtual‑call packet‑switched technology and offered X.25, SNA/SDLC, BSC and ASCII interfaces to connect host computers at thousands of large companies, educational institutions, and government agencies. It operated a large public network for dial‑up users as well as a private‑network business that allowed agencies and large firms (mainly banks and airlines) to build dedicated networks, often linked via gateways to the public network or to other public networks across the U.S. and abroad.
expected: an international data communications network headquartered in San Jose, CA
